leads.industry: Digital marketing / SEO agency (higher-ed & healthcare focus)
leads.why_pain: Actively hiring an SEO Coordinator 'skilled at project management' plus account managers and SEO specialists — coordinator roles at a 30-person shop are a direct signal of manual campaign-coordination and reporting workload. Ranked among America's fastest-growing private companies six years running, so client volume is outpacing ops.
leads.evidence: https://www.searchinfluence.com/jobs/ and https://jobs.searchinfluence.com/ (SEO Coordinator, AM, specialist openings); growth claim on company profile via search results
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at searchinfluence.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Replace the 'coordinator tax': AI-assisted SEO reporting and campaign-status automation so the team ships client updates without hiring another coordinator seat.
leads.personalization: Reference the open SEO Coordinator posting (project-management-heavy JD) and six straight years on fastest-growing lists — offer to automate the exact checklist/reporting work described in the JD.

leads.industry: Full-service digital marketing agency (SEO, paid media, social)
leads.why_pain: Careers page shows simultaneous openings for Paid Media Account Manager, Paid Media Account Director, and SEO Account Manager, with the paid media JD explicitly describing 'an agency in growth mode' — multiple client-facing hires at once means onboarding, reporting, and account-handover load is spiking.
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at passion.digital; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Growth-mode agencies burn new AM hires on manual reporting — automate client reporting and onboarding packs so each new AM carries more accounts from week one.
leads.personalization: Cite their own 'agency in growth mode' phrasing from the Paid Media AM job ad; pitch AI onboarding so the three concurrent client-facing hires ramp faster.

leads.industry: SEO & digital consultancy (ecommerce and B2B SEO)
leads.why_pain: Join Us page shows hiring for SEO Executives ('hands-on execution across on-page, technical and content SEO') and a Sales & Marketing Assistant to 'support POLARIS' growth' — junior execution + admin hires signal manual delivery and lead-gen ops. Won Best SEO Campaign at the European Search Awards (Dec 2025), so inbound demand is likely rising.
leads.evidence: https://www.polarisagency.com/join-us/ (open roles); European Search Awards win cited on polarisagency.com; size per https://whitehat-seo.co.uk/blog/18-best-seo-agencies-london-2026-pricing-honest-comparison
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at polarisagency.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Post-award demand spike: automate proposal generation and SEO reporting so the small senior team converts award-driven inbound without drowning execs in decks.
leads.personalization: Congratulate on the Dec 2025 European Search Awards win, then connect it to the two open junior roles — offer automation as the alternative to stacking admin headcount.

leads.location: San Francisco, CA, USA (fully remote team)
leads.industry: Performance marketing agency (SEM, paid social, SEO, content) for SaaS/tech
leads.why_pain: Fully remote, remote-always boutique that is frequently hiring (live job boards on Ashby and Workable). Remote-first delivery across SEM/SEO/content for demanding SaaS clients means heavy async reporting and client-communication overhead. ASSUMPTION: no dedicated ops tooling team at this size.
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at jordandigitalmarketing.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Remote-first agencies live and die by async client comms — AI-generated weekly client reports and a searchable internal knowledge system fit their remote-always operating model.
leads.personalization: Reference their 'remote-first, remote-always' positioning and JDM Impressions employee series; pitch an internal AI knowledge base as the remote culture's missing layer.

leads.industry: Performance/growth marketing agency (PPC, paid social, CRO) for B2B & consumer brands
leads.size_hint: Small team, roughly 10-25 (Glassdoor shows 21 reviews; 'fractional growth team' positioning)
leads.why_pain: Growing since 2017 with recent leadership appointments including an Operations Manager (Meg Atkin) — a dedicated ops hire is a direct admission that delivery coordination outgrew ad-hoc process. Careers page says they are 'always on the hunt' for talent; remote/flex work supported.
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at farsiight.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Give the new Operations Manager leverage: automate client onboarding and performance reporting so ops scales without more coordination headcount — APAC timezone overlap with Bali is a bonus.
leads.personalization: Reference the Ops Manager appointment and Smart50 Workplaces ranking; note Bali-Brisbane timezone alignment for a live working session.

leads.industry: SEO / ecommerce growth agency (SEO, web, digital PR, CRO)
leads.why_pain: Fully-remote team that expanded from pure SEO into ecommerce growth services in 2024 (web design, digital PR, CRO) and has been hiring a Senior SEO Specialist and a Social Media Account Manager — service-line expansion plus remote delivery multiplies onboarding and reporting variants. ASSUMPTION: founder-led sales means proposals are a founder bottleneck.
leads.evidence: https://www.clickslice.co.uk/careers/ ; hiring posts on https://www.linkedin.com/company/clicksliceseo ; expansion noted in https://agencyanalytics.com/blog/agency-profile-clickslice ; size per Whitehat London agency comparison
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at clickslice.co.uk; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: New service lines mean new deliverable formats — AI proposal automation and unified cross-service client reporting so the ecommerce pivot doesn't fragment delivery.
leads.personalization: Reference the 2024 pivot to ecommerce growth agency and the founder's Udemy SEO course scale (100k+ students) — he clearly values leverage and systemization.

leads.why_pain: Fully remote, SaaS-only agency running SEO, PPC, content, and link building for data-hungry B2B SaaS clients — multi-service delivery to metrics-driven clients means constant reporting and status communication. Already markets AI SEO services, so leadership is bought into AI. ASSUMPTION: internal ops automation lags their client-facing AI offer.
leads.evidence: https://clutch.co/profile/simpletiger-0-0 (size, reviews); https://www.simpletiger.com/services/ai-seo-agency (AI-forward positioning); remote team noted on simpletiger.com
leads.contact_channel: Website contact/demo form at simpletiger.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: They sell AI SEO — pitch practicing what they preach internally: AI-driven client reporting and onboarding automation as a case study they can also market.
leads.personalization: Reference their AI SEO / generative engine optimization service line and fully-remote model; frame internal automation as brand-consistent.

leads.why_pain: Clients repeatedly praise 'transparency in reporting' across 100 Clutch reviews and the agency publishes open pricing ($2,000-$5,000+/mo retainers) — transparent-reporting positioning at that retainer level is labor-intensive to sustain manually. ASSUMPTION: report assembly is a significant recurring time cost per account.
leads.evidence: https://clutch.co/profile/searchbloom (100 reviews, transparency praise); pricing on searchbloom.com
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at searchbloom.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Their differentiator is transparent reporting — automate it: AI reporting pipelines that keep the 'real numbers' promise while cutting hours per account.
leads.personalization: Quote the Clutch review theme ('transparency in reporting', 'real numbers') and their A.R.T. framework; position automation as protecting the differentiator as they scale.

leads.industry: SEO & digital PR agency for established businesses
leads.why_pain: Small senior team serving enterprise-grade clients (eBay, Just Eat, BrewDog, Cambridge) on £4,000+/mo 12-month retainers — enterprise client expectations on a boutique headcount means heavy manual reporting and stakeholder comms per account. ASSUMPTION: efficiency-focused operating model (listed in a comparison highlighting compressed working weeks) makes them receptive to automation.
leads.evidence: https://typeamedia.net/ and https://typeamedia.net/seo/service/pricing/ (retainers, client list); size and profile per https://whitehat-seo.co.uk/blog/18-best-seo-agencies-london-2026-pricing-honest-comparison
leads.contact_channel: Contact form at https://typeamedia.net/contact/; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Enterprise clients, boutique team: automate enterprise-grade reporting and QBR prep so senior strategists spend hours on strategy, not deck assembly.
leads.personalization: Reference serving eBay/Just Eat/BrewDog with a 10-25 person team and their published £4k/mo retainer floor — quantify hours saved per retainer.

leads.size_hint: Small senior team (founded 2024; every account person has 5+ years experience)
leads.why_pain: Founded 2024 and already opened a New York office while serving clients across NL, BE, DE, UK, Nordics, and North America — rapid multi-market expansion on a deliberately senior-only, lean team means expensive senior hours are being spent on onboarding, proposals, and reporting. ASSUMPTION: no junior layer to absorb ops work, so automation is the only scaling lever that preserves their model.
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at thegrowthsyndicate.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Their promise is senior people only — AI ops (onboarding, proposal, reporting automation) is how a senior-only agency scales without diluting the promise with juniors.
leads.personalization: Reference the 2024 founding, the fast NY expansion, and the '5+ years on every account' promise; founders are growth/data people who publish an LLM-facing info page — speak their language.

leads.location: San Diego, CA & Raleigh, NC, USA
leads.industry: SEO & content marketing agency (Certified B Corp)
leads.why_pain: Content-driven SEO delivery across two offices for mid-market/enterprise clients — content production plus SEO reporting is among the most manual agency workflows (briefs, editorial QA, performance reporting). ASSUMPTION: two-office coordination adds internal knowledge-sharing overhead.
leads.evidence: https://nextleft.com/ (B Corp, two offices); https://clutch.co/profile/nextleft (size, 26 reviews)
leads.decision_maker_role: Founder/CEO (John McKusick) or VP of Operations
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at nextleft.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: Automate the content-SEO assembly line: AI brief generation, editorial QA checks, and client performance reporting; B Corp story angle — automation frees hours for purpose work.
leads.personalization: Reference B Corp certification and dual San Diego/Raleigh offices; propose an internal knowledge system to unify playbooks across both offices.

leads.location: Spokane, WA, USA (distributed team across North America and Europe)
leads.industry: Boutique paid media agency (paid search & paid social)
leads.why_pain: Eleven-person boutique with team members across two continents managing always-on paid media accounts — campaign reporting, pacing checks, and client updates are pure manual overhead at this size, with no room for dedicated ops staff. ASSUMPTION on internal tooling gaps.
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at ernstmedia.com; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: For a senior-only 11-person paid media shop, automated pacing/reporting digests and client-update generation directly converts to billable senior hours.
leads.personalization: Reference their own blog post on how a boutique agency works (ernstmedia.com/how-a-boutique-marketing-agency-works) — mirror their 'senior attention, no bloat' argument back as an automation case.

leads.industry: Growth marketing agency for startups and scaleups
leads.why_pain: Lean team that has served 300+ startups worldwide using data- and experiment-driven campaigns — high client turnover typical of startup clients means onboarding and proposal cycles repeat constantly on a tiny team. ASSUMPTION: heavy founder involvement in every new engagement.
leads.contact_channel: Website contact form at ammomarketing.com.au; LinkedIn company page
leads.outreach_angle: 300+ startup engagements means 300+ onboardings — productize it with AI onboarding and proposal automation; Perth timezone is directly adjacent to Bali for easy collaboration.
leads.personalization: Reference the TechCrunch profile and their experiment-driven ethos; frame the engagement itself as a growth experiment with measurable hours-saved outcomes.
